title: Cholar Dal

overview.description: A fragrant Bengali lentil curry made with chana dal, whole spices and toasted coconut. Traditionally served with luchi or rice.

ingredients: 30g desiccated coconut or fresh coconut, lightly toasted
method: Pressure cook the dal with the water and turmeric for 12–15 minutes until tender but still holding its shape.
Allow the pressure to release naturally.
Heat the ghee over a medium heat.
Add the bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom and cloves.
Add the cumin seeds, dried chilli and asafoetida.
Stir in the ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground cumin and ground coriander.
Add the cooked dal with enough cooking liquid to give a loose consistency.
Stir in the jaggery and salt.
Fold in most of the toasted coconut.
Garnish with the remaining coconut and fresh coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Unlike many Indian dals, Cholar Dal is traditionally prepared without onion or garlic and is often served during festivals and celebrations in Bengal.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook chana dal so it retains its shape, toast coconut and build flavour with whole spices.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A completely different style of dal that broadens the Indian section while using long-lasting pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: Pressure cook the dal until tender but not mushy.
Toast the coconut until lightly golden for the best flavour.
This dal should be slightly sweet rather than spicy.
The whole spices should perfume the dal without overpowering it.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently with a splash of water if it thickens.
